title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston news stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
Chianca has a background as a news editor, columnist, and music writer in Greater Boston.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox used MLB's new ABS Challenge System to overturn critical calls in their 3-0 Opening Day win over the Reds.
Roman Anthony's successful ninth-inning challenge reversed a strikeout, extended the inning, and led to additional Red Sox runs.
Each team starts with two challenges in the ABS system; a successful challenge allows the team to retain it.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer made a strong impact as a pinch-hitter, going 2-for-2 in the Red Sox's 3-0 win over Cincinnati.
Mayer, drafted fourth overall in 2021, earned a spot on Boston's Opening Day roster after a challenging rookie year.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora kept Mayer on the bench for the opener, favoring match-ups against left-hand pitching.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was saved by Campton-Thornton firefighters after cutting his leg on a solar light last month.
Firefighters applied a tourniquet and pressure to Ziggy's wound, preventing severe blood loss en route to a vet.
The quick actions of Paul Spring, Mark Roland, and Basil Sole were credited with saving Ziggy's life.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy with 89,000 miles is burning excessive oil; Subaru suggests replacing the short block costing about $4,000.
Some Subaru engines face oil consumption issues due to sticking oil control piston rings; engine flushes and PCV checks may help.
Repairing a car with excessive oil use could be a worthwhile investment considering high prices of new and used vehicles.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner in Nantucket has sold five lottery tickets winning over $1 million each since March 2024.
The diner recently sold its second $2 million lottery ticket this month, bringing total winnings to over $5 million.
The diner receives a $20,000 bonus for selling each winning ticket, according to the Mass. State Lottery Commission.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles was arraigned on charges of involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide in connection with a 2025 school bus crash.
Prosecutors allege Charles failed safety inspections and drove recklessly, contributing to the death of 5-year-old Lens Arthur Joseph.
Charles was also involved in an alleged hit-and-run in August 2025 and faces separate charges for that incident.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet struck out eight over six innings, helping the Red Sox maintain a scoreless game until the seventh inning.
Marcelo Mayer's pinch-hit double sparked a seventh-inning rally, leading to the Red Sox's 3-0 victory over the Reds.
Roman Anthony went 3 for 4, displaying excellent plate discipline and contributing to Boston's win in their season opener.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, 57, was found dead five months after disappearing from Heywood Hospital in Gardner, Massachusetts.
Mercury escaped the hospital by jumping from a window while in mental and medical distress, wearing only hospital clothes.
Gardner police located his body using drones in Crystal Lake; the cause of death is under investigation by medical authorities.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after recovering from a giant cell tumor in her femur.
More than ten years post-surgery, Southern supports Mass General Hospital, which restored her mobility, through her marathon run.
Southern aims to raise funds for Mass General Hospital, honoring the care she received during a critical time in her life.
